threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 243 bus will get you anywhere near on time—news flash, mate: it’s more a suggestion than a schedule.
Heard a guy on the 333 arguing with the driver about how the bus was haunted. Claimed it only stops for ghosts at midnight.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I spotted a kid trading Pokémon cards with an old man outside the Späti, both of them arguing over which Charizard was cooler. That's real Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a café that doubles as a tattoo parlor. Coffee and ink, all in one spot—perfect for the indecisive!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell who’s local by how they know to avoid BART on Fridays; only tourists would brave the Transbay Tube with all that smoke and chaos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 6 bus at 5th and Market, a guy serenading the crowd with a ukulele while we waited. Classic Tenderloin moment—both chaotic and kind of beautiful.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's charm is all about the plazas, but they miss the real magic in the tiny bakeries tucked away on side streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a recent arrival by the way they hesitantly order a café amb llet and still look around like they expect a barista to answer in English.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still wait for the bus like it's reliable. Locals know it’s more a suggestion than a schedule.
Sat on the 37 last night, a woman in a pink feather boa serenaded a group of bemused tourists. Classic Brixton vibes—always a show on the night bus.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a street vendor selling vegan döner at 3 AM while a group of artists painted a mural nearby. Pure Neukölln magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you catch a bus that smells like legit currywurst at 3 AM after the club. It's oddly comforting amidst the chaos.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a true local when they can name the pop-up taco truck that’s been parked outside The Abbey for the last three months.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy on the bus passionately explaining the history of the Tenderloin to a confused tourist. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always miss how locals use the quiet back alleys for shortcuts. They stick to the main streets and miss half the charm of Gràcia.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their confused look when they try to use the metro ticket machines; locals just glide through while they fumble.
